summary: Implemented the US-844 desktop score/result UI in the shared browser-remake worktree without touching domain scoring contracts, then completed a focused MZ-403 continuation to fix acceptance-review score-panel clipping. The game surface now renders an always-visible compact score panel for all players with bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist values that fit the left desktop side panel across active, settlement, next-deal, and finished states; structured current/previous deal result panels during deal-settlement and next-deal; and a finished-state final settlement plus deterministic ranking summary sourced from finalResult. The finished phase continues to expose no continuation action, satisfying the completed-bullet guard at the UI layer.

tests_run.evidence: 26 UI/controller tests passed, confirming the renderer changes did not break start/resume controller behavior.
35 integration and persistence tests passed, covering settlement transitions, next-deal flow, and save/load compatibility with finished-bullet suppression.
Vitest: 12 files and 123 tests passed. Build: tsc and vite build succeeded.
Dev server served the app at http://127.0.0.1:5331/. Playwright browser evidence captured start, game, settlement, and final states. Console showed one benign favicon.ico 404 only.

downstream_notes: No controller behavior change was needed for completed bullets because the domain/legal-action layer already exposes no finished-phase continuation and persistence already suppresses finished-bullet resume.

The compact score panel shows the current state totals, while deal-settlement and next-deal panels show the before/delta/after breakdown sourced from dealResult and previousDealResult for compact traceability.

Acceptance review reported that the original side-panel score table clipped mutual-whist columns in settlement and finished screenshots. This continuation replaced that wide table with a compact per-player score layout intended to keep all mutual-whist values readable without horizontal scrolling.

verification_environment.verification_scope: focused UI/component tests, settlement/persistence regressions, then full npm run verify

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

visual_artifacts.notes: Start-page snapshot captured from the dev server session.
Desktop start screen on the local dev server.
Active in-game desktop UI with score table visible.
Synthetic state injection used to render the settlement view deterministically.

Earlier settlement snapshot showed score information, before/delta/after result table, whist list, and only the 'Подсчитать' action, but acceptance review found the old side-panel table clipped mutual-whist columns. The current implementation replaces that wide table with a compact per-player score layout; screenshots were not retaken in this continuation.
Synthetic state injection used to render the final-result view deterministically.

Earlier final snapshot showed score information, final ranking with 3 places, final settlement text, 'Партия завершена', and no action buttons or continuation controls, but acceptance review found the old side-panel table clipped mutual-whist columns. The current implementation replaces that wide table with a compact per-player score layout; screenshots were not retaken in this continuation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Render bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist totals for all three players in a visible compact desktop score panel that does not rely on hidden horizontal scroll for the primary score information.

evidence_matrix.limitations: Browser screenshots were captured before this compact-layout continuation, so post-fix visual evidence in this report relies on the updated DOM/CSS plus fresh automated tests/build rather than replacement screenshots.
